title: CCRG iter-10: Averted-Cost Capability, Absorber Catalog, Transfer Forks, Cite Lock
layman_summary: Prepares the citations and both-outcome wording for the next draft of a paper about detecting and cataloguing unreliable "absorber" features hidden inside language-model sparse autoencoders.
summary: Pure web-research positioning ($0, no code) for iteration-10 of the CCRG paper, securing the GEN_PAPER_TEXT framing before the two NEW load-bearing experiments resolve in PARALLEL artifacts (verdicts UNKNOWN at research time, so every result-dependent passage is supplied in BOTH forks + a selector). Fixes the two publication-gating reviewer MAJORS: R2/significance (the localization+screen reposition reads as reassurance, not a build-on capability) and R1/evidence (the iter-9 where-to-gate win was circular: a full-label-discovered absorber id vs an n-label-restricted dense gate, discovery cost never netted). S1 grounds M1''''' AVERTED-COST CAPABILITY + ABSORBER CATALOG: a four-beat scenario (parent recall hole -> standard top-N marginal-attribution selection (SCR/TPP/SHIFT, Karvonen 2411.18895 / Marks 2403.19647 ICLR2025) silently drops the absorber by construction -> the label-free firing-signature screen flags the hole and names the absorber, succeeding where a contested decoder-cosine proxy misses it (Georgia cos~0.012, oracle 27/31) and where the field's own audit (Chanin 'Are SAE Benchmarks Reliable?' 2605.18229: TPP/SCR 'should not be used') calls the proxy unreliable -> re-adding the absorber repairs with measured benefit) + the absorber CATALOG as a feature-level KG whose INTERVENTIONAL recall-hole edges are invisible to Winnicki et al.'s OBSERVATIONAL co-occurrence/transcoder-geometry KG-from-SAE (2604.23829); both forks AVERTED_COST_CONFIRMED/DETECTION_ONLY (catalog + detection argument UNCONDITIONAL). S2 grounds M2''''' genuine TRANSFER / break-even K* in both forks (TRANSFER_CONFIRMED real per-REDEPLOYMENT saving via fixed-id zero-label reuse vs per-deployment-refit dense gate / DROP -> stand on M1'''''+localization) on the carried label-efficiency/AL/all-supervised-gated-steering anchors, with the n=5-dense honest comparison, n=1-construction-artifact caveat, and selection-INDEPENDENT behavioral-KL (R4) baked in. S3 supplies results-first section openers (per fork), an appendix-changelog migration block, and a FINAL 12-item presentation-strip checklist (R5 de-scaffold folding in R3/R4/R6). S4 carries the iter-6/7/8/9 venue-locked table [1]-[23] VERBATIM and adds the three pre-verified new cites [24]-[26] with BibTeX + an unresolved-flags list. Outputs research_out.json + research_report.md (S1-S4).
answer:
This iteration-10 positioning pass finalizes the CCRG paper framing for GEN_PAPER_TEXT so the draft is coherent WHICHEVER way the two make-or-break experiments land. M1''''' (averted-cost capability + absorber catalog) and M2''''' (genuine transfer / break-even K*) run in PARALLEL artifacts whose verdicts are UNKNOWN at research time, so every result-dependent passage is supplied in BOTH fork variants plus an explicit selector (as iter-8 OUTCOME-A/B and iter-9 FORK-A/B). The two pieces target the two reviewer MAJORS gating publication: R2/significance (the localization + confinement-screen reposition reads as reassurance, not a capability others build on) and R1/evidence (the iter-9 label-scarce where-to-gate win was exposed as circular). All full drop-in prose, openers, the checklist, and BibTeX live in research_report.md (S1-S4).

=== S1: AVERTED-COST CAPABILITY + ABSORBER CATALOG (dominant-blocker / R2 fix) ===
The screen is converted from a reassurance instrument into a build-on capability via a four-beat scenario, each beat literature-grounded. (a) Absorption gives the parent latent a recall hole on the absorbed sub-context [23], so a parent-latent downstream artifact silently fails on that slice. (b) STANDARD PRACTICE does not flag it: the canonical SAE feature-selection routines rank latents by MARGINAL attribution to a concept probe and keep the top-N. Verbatim from Marks et al. SHIFT/Sparse-Feature-Circuits (ICLR 2025) [26]: nodes are kept by 'filter for nodes with |IE(m; a)| > T_N', an explicit threshold on a marginal indirect-effect score averaged over the concept distribution; and from Karvonen/Rager/Marks/Nanda SCR+TPP [25]: 'For both SCR and TPP, we select SAE latents using probe attribution scores' and 'We select the top N latents with the highest attribution scores' (score I(a,c)=(d_a.P)(a_pos-a_neg)). The structural claim (theorem-free, respecting the avoid-CL-theory constraint): an absorber fires only on its narrow slice, so its broad-distribution class-mean gap (a_pos-a_neg) is small, so its attribution score is small, so it falls below the top-N cut / threshold T_N and is dropped BY CONSTRUCTION; the parent (which carries the hole) is the one selected and the one that silently underperforms on the slice. (c) THE LOAD-BEARING ARGUMENT 'a contested proxy fails exactly where the firing-signature screen succeeds', two pillars: (i) the field's own reliability proxies are contested -- Chanin 'Are SAE Benchmarks Reliable?' [8] finds TPP and SCR 'fail multiple lenses ... should not be used to evaluate SAEs' -- and TPP/SCR are the very metrics that operationalize the standard-practice selection, so the screen fills a gap the field's own audit declared open; (ii) the concrete worked example -- the decoder-cosine ORACLE proxy MISSES Georgia's absorber (cos ~0.012, below the cos>0.025 absorption threshold of the supervised diagnostic [15]), corroborated on 27/31 of the validation sample with Georgia among the few where the geometric proxy disagrees, while the firing-signature screen CATCHES it. (d) repair-with-measured-benefit grounds on Peng 'discover, not act' [11] (the screen discovers the absorber + the silent failure) and Scaling Monosemanticity [18] (a concentrated feature is a sharp conditional gate = a clean repair handle). THE CATALOG / FEATURE-KG 'why build on it': the catalog is a label-free-derivable table <concept, sub-context, parent latent, absorber latent, oracle-corroboration> over public Gemma Scope SAEs = a feature-level knowledge graph, placing the contribution in the goal's KG/knowledge-extraction scope [22]. DELTA anchored to Winnicki et al. [24] (the closest prior work, 'reframes a flat SAE inventory as an internal knowledge graph'): Winnicki's edges are OBSERVATIONAL ('a co-occurrence graph ... and a transcoder-based mechanism graph that links source-layer and target-layer features through sparse latent pathways'); our catalog edges are INTERVENTIONAL firing-signature absorption structure (recall-hole + firing-disjoint + precision), encoding the absorbed-child SPECIALIZATION that co-occurrence/geometry is blind to by construction (parent/absorber are firing-disjoint -> low co-occurrence; Georgia decoder-cos ~0.012 -> low geometric similarity). The two KG views are complementary, not competing. Gallifant [10] (SAE features transfer 2B->9B) supports cross-model auditing value of a mined catalog (NOT a beats-dense claim). BOTH FORKS: AVERTED_COST_CONFIRMED (repair beats unrepaired baseline, bootstrap CI excludes 0 on >=1 sub-context -> 'actionable reliability tool with measurable averted cost') / DETECTION_ONLY (detection lands but repair does not cleanly beat baseline, consistent with the iter-9 downstream-capability null where a dense probe out-recalls the repaired unit on 4/5 concepts -> 'label-free detection/audit tool + catalog'). SELECTOR: AVERTED_COST_CONFIRMED iff repair CI excludes 0 on >=1 absorbed sub-context, else DETECTION_ONLY; the catalog deliverable and the contested-proxy-fails detection argument are UNCONDITIONAL in BOTH forks.

=== S2: TRANSFER / BREAK-EVEN, BOTH FORKS (R1/evidence fix) ===
iter-9 was circular (SAE handle reused absorber ids discovered with FULL labels + oracle, compared against an n-label-restricted dense gate; saving never netted against discovery cost; amortization never exercised). M2''''' fixes this two ways: (1) TRANSFER -- discover the absorber id ONCE, apply the FIXED id with ZERO new labels to a DIFFERENT deployment of the SAME sub-context (held-out distribution / corpus split / carrier-template shift / paired model variant) and beat an n-label dense gate fit FRESH there; or (2) NET-OUT + BREAK-EVEN -- account for discovery-time label cost, reframe as amortizable over K>=K* redeployments, REPORT K*. Carried anchors and roles: Whitney [2] = empirical loss-data-curve / break-even vocabulary (EXPLICITLY NOT a sample-complexity theorem); Im&Li [3] = diff-of-means strongest fair dense comparator, built from LABELED pairs (the per-deployment label numerator the SAE route avoids); Tan/Chanin [4] = steering/gate directions dataset-dependent + brittle, so a fresh n-label gate degrades at low n AND across deployment shift (the axis transfer exploits: the dense gate must be REFIT per deployment, paying labels each time); Rauch [5]/PEAL[6,vision]/PALP[7] = AL reduces but never removes the label cost; CAST [19]/GSS [20]/GUARD-IT/SADI = gated-steering prior art ALL SUPERVISED, so label-free where-to-gate discovery is the only SAE-specific delta and it only exists IF transfer / a small K* lands. BOTH FORKS: TRANSFER_CONFIRMED (fixed-id zero-label SAE gate's CI separates above an n-label dense gate refit fresh on a genuinely different deployment at n in {1,5} -> real per-REDEPLOYMENT saving, stated per-redeployment not per-sub-context to avoid the iter-9 circularity) / DROP (fair n-label dense gate matches by n=5 and no small K* holds once discovery cost is netted -> DROP the where-to-gate thesis; paper stands on M1'''''+localization; an honest publishable negative). SELECTOR: TRANSFER_CONFIRMED iff (transfer arm) CI separates above n-label dense at n in {1,5} on a DIFFERENT deployment OR (break-even arm) a netted K* is small (K* <= single-digit redeployments); else DROP; iter-8/9 prior (fair gate matched at full labels) flags DROP as the realistic baseline expectation, not presumed. HONEST-COMPARISON NOTES baked into both forks: report the n=5 dense point (iter-9 n=5 was within ~0.03 balanced-accuracy of the SAE handle); flag the n=1 dense collapse as PARTLY a diff-means-midpoint gate-CONSTRUCTION artifact not pure label starvation; lean the localization axis on the selection-INDEPENDENT next-token behavioral-KL (sub-context vs siblings) + held-out generalization (R4).

=== S3: R5 DE-SCAFFOLD (results-first openers + appendix migration + 12-item checklist) ===
Drop-in results-first openers (one per fork where result-dependent) for: where-to-gate/transfer, repair-spine ('beats the two informative label-free selectors S-mag and S-rec at FDR<=0.05 on 16/24 holes'), averted-cost, catalog/feature-KG, localization (selectivity=localization not surgical-advantage; fair collateral 2.79e-6 < KG 5.07e-5; adv_KG_vs_FAIR ~ -0.05 CI incl 0; +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] on 'large' vs strongest ungated dense), and confinement-screen (6/110=5.5% Wilson[0.025,0.114]). Appendix-changelog migration block: move EVERY 'the prior version conceded/'a reviewer noted'/'+1.58 was inflated'/'Georgia +0.561 retracted'/iteration-rebuttal sentence into the appendix changelog; present every corrected number AS a result (report +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] directly, never 'corrected down from +1.58'); a find-and-replace phrase list is provided. FINAL 12-item presentation-strip checklist (exactly 12): (1) one stable name 'recall-hole-anchored absorber localization', strip clustering/grouping/CCRG; (2) R5 corrected-results-AS-results + scaffolding-to-appendix + open each section with its result; (3) R3 'beats S-mag and S-rec at FDR<=0.05 on 16/24 holes', parent-argmax controls VACUOUS-BY-CONSTRUCTION not 'four stronger controls', precision_specific=False, non-triviality 6/7 numeric; (4) R4 balanced-accuracy~firing-precision-selection coincidence stated, lean on held-out generalization + selection-independent behavioral-KL; (5) R6 Amazon adv_joint-vs-adv_pres instrument disagreement diagnosed else report both + soften 'demonstrated'; (6) selectivity-as-LOCALIZATION not surgical-advantage, 16k denominator=DISOWNED whole-parent strawman, fair collateral 2.79e-6 < KG 5.07e-5, adv_KG_vs_FAIR ~ -0.05 CI incl 0, adv_KG_vs_SUB +0.97; (7) lead edit = KG vs strongest UNGATED dense = +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] on 'large', never +1.58; (8) carry spine 24 holes (supersedes 22)/30 FDR, 16k 1452x/1262x, 65k median ~676x (never 466997x divide-by-eps), cross-dict 65k FULL/layer-9 PARTIAL, member-labeling 0.730 vs 0.096, model-diffing +0.000; (9) homograph-confinement HEADLINE 6/110=5.5%, safety 2/44 both homographs, named-entity 3/5, religion/ethnicity/months never structured, 0/28 professions; (10) clustering INERT (0/8) + all three goal-named downstream tasks NULLS reported as findings; (11) concentration-not-absorption is the edit-win predictor, firing-signature != edit-handle; (12) lead with the NEW frame (averted-cost capability + absorber catalog) as headline 'why build on it', where-to-gate net saving CONTINGENT on the M2''''' fork.

=== S4: FINAL CITE LOCK ===
The iter-6/7/8/9 venue-verified table [1]-[23] is carried VERBATIM (re-confirmed the gated-steering quartet CAST 2409.05907 ICLR2025-Spotlight / GSS 2602.08901 / GUARD-IT 2605.12765 / SADI 2410.12299 ICLR2025; a quartet-completeness note instructs GEN_PAPER_TEXT to keep GUARD-IT + SADI in the bibliography). THREE new cites re-confirmed this iteration with verbatim grounding: [24] Winnicki/Gnanasekaran/Darve 2604.23829 (cs.AI preprint v1 26 Apr 2026 / v2 28 Apr 2026; observational co-occurrence + transcoder-geometry KG-from-SAE) -- the catalog/feature-KG contrast; [25] Karvonen/Rager/Marks/Nanda 2411.18895 (cs.LG/cs.CL preprint 28 Nov 2024, no venue yet; SCR + TPP marginal-attribution selection) -- the standard practice that silently drops absorbers; [26] Marks/Rager/Michaud/Belinkov/Bau/Mueller 2403.19647 (ICLR 2025, journal-ref confirmed on the arXiv abstract page; OpenReview I4e82CIDxv; SHIFT) -- the attribution-thresholded-selection origin. BibTeX for all three (two @misc preprints + one @inproceedings ICLR2025) and an unresolved-flags list (new: Winnicki preprint, Karvonen SCR/TPP standalone-paper no-venue; carried: Rauch author count, CorrSteer ICML-vs-ICLR, SAEBench ICML-vs-ICLR, upgrade [2]/[3]/[8]/GUARD-IT/GSS if venues appear) are in S4.3/S4.4.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on the three new cite IDs/authors/venues (grep-verified from arXiv abstract + body pages this pass: Winnicki 2604.23829 author list + observational co-occurrence/transcoder phrasing; Karvonen 2411.18895 'select the top N latents with the highest attribution scores'; Marks 2403.19647 journal-ref ICLR 2025 + 'filter for nodes with |IE(m; a)| > T_N'); HIGH on the carried-table contents (read directly from the iter-9 research_out.json + iter-7/8 research_report.md). MEDIUM only on inherited venue flags (CorrSteer, SAEBench, Rauch author count) and the no-venue status of [25], all flagged for camera-ready. The single largest uncertainty is exogenous: the M1''''' and M2''''' verdicts, which is why both forks + selectors are mandatory.

sources.title: Negative Results for Sparse Autoencoders On Downstream Tasks and Deprioritising SAE Research (DeepMind Mechanistic Interpretability Team)
sources.summary: CARRIED [1]. Mar 26 2025 progress update. Dense linear probes 'perform nearly perfectly, including out of distribution'; k-sparse SAE probes 'distinctly worse on the OOD set'; team DEPRIORITISES SAE research, pivots to model diffing. Predicts the classification/probing null AND independently flags model-diffing (our +0.000 task) as the hard open direction.

sources.title: Evaluating representations by the complexity of learning low-loss predictors (Whitney, Song, Brandfonbrener, Altosaar, Cho)
sources.summary: CARRIED [2]. Loss-data curve, Surplus Description Length, epsilon-sample-complexity for how much labeled data a representation needs to hit a target loss. Grounds the M2''''' quality-vs-#labels CURVE methodology and the 'empirical label budget / break-even' vocabulary -- explicitly empirical, NOT a theorem (respects avoid-CL-theory). Preprint (OpenReview FOR2VqgJXb).

sources.title: A Unified Understanding and Evaluation of Steering Methods (Im & Li)
sources.summary: CARRIED [3]. Mean-of-differences (difference-of-means) is the provably-optimal and empirically strongest steering vector. Justifies u_sub=diff-of-means as the strongest FAIR dense comparator AND establishes that building it needs per-deployment LABELED contrast pairs -- the per-redeployment label numerator the SAE route avoids. Preprint.

sources.title: Analysing the Generalisation and Reliability of Steering Vectors (Tan, Chanin, Lynch, Paige, Kanoulas, Garriga-Alonso, Kirk)
sources.summary: CARRIED [4]. NeurIPS 2024. Steerability is 'mostly a property of the dataset rather than the model', highly variable, brittle OOD -- so a supervised gate degrades with its label budget AND must be refit across deployment shift (the axis the transfer test exploits). David Chanin co-author (reliability through-line with [8],[15]).

sources.title: No Free Lunch in Active Learning: LLM Embedding Quality Dictates Query Strategy Success (Rauch, Wirth, Huseljic, Herde, Sick)
sources.summary: CARRIED [5]. Benchmarks deep active learning over FROZEN LLM embeddings on 10 text-classification tasks; optimal query strategy is sensitive to embedding quality -- AL REDUCES but never removes the label requirement. The LLM-domain anchor for 'the labeled gate's cost can be reduced by AL yet still needs labels; the SAE route needs none'. Preprint; verify author count.

sources.title: Parameter-Efficient Active Learning for Foundational models (Lakshmi Narayanan, Krishnan, Subedar)
sources.summary: CARRIED [6]. CVPR 2024 Transformers-for-Vision Workshop (cs.CV -- VISION modality FLAG). AL + LoRA over frozen-feature linear probes. Secondary AL anchor; cite ONLY for the active-learning-reduces-but-needs-labels principle, never as same-domain (text/LLM) evidence (use [5] for that).

sources.title: Prompt-Augmented Linear Probing: Scaling beyond the Limit of Few-shot In-Context Learners (Cho, Kim, Kim, Kim, Lee, Yoo)
sources.summary: CARRIED [7]. AAAI 2023. Hybrid of linear probing and in-context learning that inherits linear probing's scalability in labeled samples. Secondary few-shot-linear-probing label-efficiency backdrop for S2.

sources.title: Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable? (Chanin)
sources.summary: CARRIED [8]. Preprint, sole author David Chanin. Audits SAEBench via three lenses; TPP and SCR 'fail multiple lenses ... should not be used to evaluate SAEs'; even sae-probes cannot separate SAE variants; 'the field needs better SAE benchmarks'. The LOAD-BEARING S1.1 pillar (i): the field's own proxy metrics -- and TPP/SCR specifically -- are unreliable, so a task-grounded label-free oracle-validated screen fills a gap the field declared open.

sources.title: Rethinking Evaluation of Sparse Autoencoders through the Representation of Polysemous Words (Minegishi, Furuta, Iwasawa, Matsuo)
sources.summary: CARRIED [9]. ICLR 2025. Evaluates SAEs via polysemous words; MSE-L0-Pareto improvements can CONFUSE interpretability. Grounds the LEXICAL-POLYSEMY framing of the confinement finding -- absorption/reliability is fundamentally a polysemy phenomenon, so confinement to homograph/spelling polysemy is the expected shape.

sources.title: Sparse Autoencoder Features for Classifications and Transferability (Gallifant, Chen, Sasse, Aerts, Hartvigsen, Bitterman)
sources.summary: CARRIED [10]. EMNLP 2025 (arXiv 2502.11367). SAE-derived features reach macro-F1>0.8 and transfer from a smaller to a larger model (Gemma 2 2B->9B-IT). Cite for the cross-model auditing use-case the catalog serves (smaller-model SAE structure predicts larger-model behaviour); NOT a beats-dense claim (it beats hidden-state/BoW under POOLING, which does not contradict localization-not-classification).

sources.title: Use Sparse Autoencoders to Discover Unknown Concepts, Not to Act on Known Concepts (Peng, Movva, Kleinberg, Pierson, Garg)
sources.summary: CARRIED [11]. Verbatim thesis: SAEs are 'powerful tools for discovering unknown concepts' but 'less effective for acting on known concepts'. The constructive spine -- the screen DISCOVERS the absorber + the silent failure (beat d); the DETECTION_ONLY 'discover-not-act' frame.

sources.title: AxBench: Steering LLMs (Wu et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED [12]. ICML 2025. Simple baselines (difference-of-means) beat SAEs on detection and beat SAE steering on aggregate. Predicts the steering null and grounds localization-not-classification; the side-effect/fluency LLM-judge eval bar the edit arm borrows.

sources.title: SAEBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Sparse Autoencoders (Karvonen et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED [13]. ICML 2025 (OpenReview qrU3yNfX0d). The SAE evaluation suite (includes feature-absorption + SCR/TPP sparse-probing metrics) that Chanin [8] audits and finds unreliable. Reconfirm ICML-vs-ICLR at bib-time. Note: the SCR/TPP metrics originate in [25].

sources.title: SAEs Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
sources.summary: CARRIED [14]. ICLR 2025. Raw SAE latents are not canonical units (feature splitting/absorption). Motivates why 'build a more reliable unit' was the right question and why a single canonical multi-member answer should not be expected -- backs the clustering-null finding.

sources.title: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED [15]. NeurIPS 2025. The SUPERVISED, spelling-bound absorption diagnostic (LR probe on first-letter labels -> max-encoder-cosine parent; cosine>0.025 threshold; ablation on the first-letter logit + probe-projection). The DELTA reference for the label-free recall-hole anchoring; the cos>0.025 threshold the Georgia cos~0.012 miss falls below.

sources.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering -- If You Select the Right Features (Arad, Mueller, Belinkov)
sources.summary: CARRIED [16]. EMNLP 2025 (arXiv 2505.20063). SAEs become competitive with supervised steering only AFTER filtering to the right (specific, high-output-score) features; clean handles are a minority. Predicts the steering null; supports concentration-not-absorption + localization-not-classification.

sources.title: Can SAEs reveal and mitigate racial biases of LLMs in healthcare? (Ahsan & Wallace)
sources.summary: CARRIED [17]. ICLR 2026. Steers a single race-correlated SAE latent that CO-FIRES on the attribute and 'incarceration' (entanglement, not a clean absorber) and concedes SAE steering is 'of marginal utility for realistic tasks'. Corroborates the homograph-confined safety null -- identity attributes are co-firing, not absorption-structured.

sources.title: Scaling Monosemanticity (Templeton et al., Anthropic 2024)
sources.summary: CARRIED [18]. Specificity = 'when the feature is active, the relevant concept is reliably present'. Grounds beat (d): a concentrated/specific absorber is a sharp conditional gate, hence a clean repair handle; and the safety-attribute-auditing use-case the catalog serves.

sources.title: Programming Refusal with Conditional Activation Steering / CAST (Lee et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED [19]. ICLR 2025 Spotlight. Gating a steering edit by a SUPERVISED condition vector fit on labeled example sets. The venue-backed anchor that the gating OPERATOR is established prior art -- so the SAE-specific value can only be where the gate is discovered; the editing caveat in both M2''''' forks.

sources.title: GSS: Gated Subspace Steering (Zhang, Shang, Li)
sources.summary: CARRIED [20]. Preprint. Publishes essentially the exact gated-dense operator h'=h-G(|u^T h|>eps)v with probe/steer OPTIMIZED on labeled sequences. Confirms the gated-dense control is prior art and supervised; the editing-is-not-a-novel-mechanism caveat; one of the four all-supervised gated-steering methods.

sources.title: Beyond Interpretability: Label-Free Visual Steering (Chatzoudis et al.)
sources.summary: CARRIED [21]. Preprint (cs.CV). Trains a top-k SAE on unlabeled activations and steers without labels; establishes 'no labels needed' as a recognized value axis. Supports the label-free-discovery spine of both M2''''' forks (modality note: vision).

sources.title: ICLR 2026 Call for Papers
sources.summary: CARRIED [22]. Lists 'societal considerations including fairness, safety, privacy' and 'visualization or interpretation of learned representations' -- clean area fit for the localization + feature-KG/catalog + label-free-screen framing. ICLR primary per goal; ICML fallback.

sources.title: Toy Models of Feature Absorption in SAEs (LessWrong)
sources.summary: CARRIED [23]. Defines absorption as 'gerrymandered SAE latents ... with holes in their recall' -- the recall-hole concept the localization procedure and beat (a) anchor on. Defensive novelty: using the recall hole as the selection anchor is the new step.

sources.title: Domain-Filtered Knowledge Graphs from Sparse Autoencoder Features (Winnicki, Gnanasekaran, Darve)
sources.summary: NEW [24]. arXiv 2604.23829, cs.AI, preprint (v1 26 Apr 2026, v2 28 Apr 2026), no venue. Verbatim: builds 'a co-occurrence graph for corpus-level conceptual structure ... and a transcoder-based mechanism graph that links source-layer and target-layer features through sparse latent pathways', automated edge labeling; 'reframes a flat SAE inventory as an internal knowledge graph'. The OBSERVATIONAL KG-from-SAE contrast for the catalog delta (S1.2): our INTERVENTIONAL recall-hole/firing-disjoint edges are invisible to co-occurrence (firing-disjoint -> low co-occurrence) and to weight-geometry (Georgia decoder-cos ~0.012).

sources.title: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks (Karvonen, Rager, Marks, Nanda)
sources.summary: NEW [25]. arXiv 2411.18895, cs.LG/cs.CL, preprint (28 Nov 2024), no venue yet (flag). Introduces the automated SHIFT/Spurious Correlation Removal (SCR) metric and Targeted Probe Perturbation (TPP). Verbatim: 'For both SCR and TPP, we select SAE latents using probe attribution scores' and 'We select the top N latents with the highest attribution scores' (score I(a,c)=(d_a.P)(a_pos-a_neg)). The 'standard practice' that ranks by MARGINAL attribution and silently drops absorbers by construction (S1.1 beats a/b); and one of the TPP/SCR metrics Chanin [8] finds unreliable.

sources.title: Sparse Feature Circuits: Discovering and Editing Interpretable Causal Graphs in Language Models (Marks, Rager, Michaud, Belinkov, Bau, Mueller)
sources.summary: NEW [26]. arXiv 2403.19647, Journal reference: International Conference on Learning Representations 2025 (ICLR 2025, confirmed on the arXiv abstract page; OpenReview I4e82CIDxv). Introduces SHIFT (improve classifier generalization by ablating features a human judges task-irrelevant). Node selection is thresholded marginal attribution: verbatim 'for each node a in G and input x~D, we compute IE(m; a; x); we then average over x~D to produce a score IE(m; a) and filter for nodes with |IE(m; a)| > T_N'. The attribution-thresholded-selection origin where a dropped absorber silently fails (S1.1); also the SHIFT spurious-cue-removal task.

follow_up_questions: Which M1''''' fork landed -- AVERTED_COST_CONFIRMED (named-absorber repair beats the unrepaired practitioner baseline, bootstrap CI excludes 0 on >=1 absorbed sub-context) or DETECTION_ONLY (detection lands but repair does not cleanly beat the baseline)? What is the measured repair-benefit CI on each absorbed sub-context, and does the absorber catalog ship as a standalone feature-KG deliverable regardless?

Which M2''''' fork landed -- TRANSFER_CONFIRMED (fixed-id zero-label SAE gate's CI separates above an n-label dense gate refit fresh on a genuinely different deployment at n in {1,5}; report the beaten n) or break-even-K* (report K* and whether K* <= a single-digit number of redeployments) or DROP (fair dense gate matches by n=5, no realized SAE-specific where-to-gate saving)?

Camera-ready cite resolution: did a venue appear for Winnicki 2604.23829, Karvonen SCR/TPP 2411.18895 (standalone), Chanin-reliability 2605.18229, Whitney 2009.07368, Im&Li 2502.02716, GUARD-IT 2605.12765, or GSS 2602.08901; is CorrSteer ICML-2026 or ICLR-2026; is SAEBench ICML-vs-ICLR; and is the Rauch 2506.01992 author count/order (5: Rauch, Wirth, Huseljic, Herde, Sick) correct?
